name: Eleanor Bradford — The Second American Civil War: New England
description:
(For @JimmyCarter)

The year is 1936, and the word is a different place that it was 20 years ago. In 1922, the Central Powers triumphed over the fatigued Entente Powers, resulting in Prussian might to dominate both Europe and geopolitical affairs. Mainland Britain and France fell into Syndicalism, the Bolsheviks collapse under the White Army's victory, and the United States finds itself more isolated than before. By the time of the 1936 elections, the polarization of American politics has left both the national and the government paralyzed.

Then, on March 4th, 1937, the unthinkable, yet inevitable happens. General Douglas MacArthur of the US Army marches his forces into Washington DC and declares martial law as a junta is established with emergency powers. The Second American Civil War begins.

Among the soldiers that make up the soldiers within New England' "Defense Army", is Eleanor Bradford, an anthro female red fox from Boston who took up arms and now rallies behind the New England flag due to her family being to young or old to do so.

(Image Source: Myself)

⬇️ Backstory Below: ⬇️

==================

Eleanor Bradford inhales the salt air of Boston Harbor, a perfect, crisp autumn wind rushing through the trees of Beacon Hill. The year is 1924, and life in her family's Back Bay brownstone is a study in gilded normalcy. She is 12, her life mapped out by society pages and debutante balls, yet her mind is always miles away, across the Atlantic.

The world map is wrong.

Europe is not where history left it. It is a continent cleaved, a monument to the unexpected German victory decades ago. The German Empire's Reichspakt dominates the European geopolitics, a vast yellow-and-black stain of industrial might. But further west,, the Internationale—the scarlet tide of syndicalist Britain and France—stirs constant, feverish revolution.
